name: Pishacha
description: Pishachas are demonic, flesh-eating beings in Hindu mythology. They are often described as grotesque and terrifying creatures, associated with graveyards, cremation grounds, and desolate places. Pishachas are known for their ability to shapeshift, often taking on the forms of animals or humans to deceive and torment their victims. They are believed to feed on human flesh and blood, and are attracted to impure or unholy environments. They are considered to be malevolent spirits, capable of causing illness, madness, and even death. Pishachas are often depicted as having dark skin, bulging eyes, and sharp claws. They are said to be the offspring of Daksha's daughters, Pisacha and Krodha. They are generally considered lower in the demonic hierarchy than Rakshasas, but are still a significant threat. Mantras, rituals, and devotion to deities can ward them off. Their existence serves as a reminder of the importance of purity, righteousness, and spiritual discipline. They embody negativity, decay, and the dangers of succumbing to base desires. The Pishacha represents the darker aspects of human nature and the consequences of straying from the path of dharma. They are a cautionary figure, highlighting the need for spiritual awareness and protection against negative influences.
subtitle: Flesh-eating demons of Hindu lore
appearance: Pishachas are described as having a grotesque appearance, often with dark skin, bulging eyes, sharp claws, and disfigured features. They may appear emaciated or bloated. Their forms are often unstable and shifting, reflecting their chaotic nature. Some accounts depict them with long, matted hair and decaying flesh.
habitat: Pishachas are typically found in graveyards, cremation grounds, desolate forests, and other impure or unholy places. They are attracted to environments associated with death, decay, and negativity. They are also said to inhabit the fringes of human settlements, preying on the vulnerable and unwary.
behavior: Pishachas are malevolent and predatory creatures. They are known for their cruelty, deceitfulness, and lust for flesh. They enjoy tormenting humans and are said to cause illness, madness, and death. They are attracted to negative emotions and impure thoughts. Pishachas are also known to disturb religious rituals and desecrate sacred sites.

keyMyths.title: Origin of Pishachas
keyMyths.description: Pishachas are said to be the offspring of Daksha's daughters, Pisacha and Krodha. This lineage connects them to anger and monstrous tendencies. Their birth signifies the emergence of darkness and decay in the world.

keyMyths.title: Pishachas and Cremation Grounds
keyMyths.description: Cremation grounds are considered a favorite haunt of Pishachas. They feed on the decaying flesh and are drawn to the powerful energies associated with death and transition. Rituals are performed to ward them off from these sacred spaces.

sources.author: Swami Sivananda
